subject: Physics
title: The Steam Engine Revolution
description: A 60-second lesson on The Steam Engine Revolution.

hook.text: The steam engine turned heat into motion and launched the Industrial Revolution, reshaping human civilization.

buildup.text: Thomas Newcomen built the first practical steam engine in 1712 to pump water from coal mines.

discovery.text: James Watt improved it dramatically in the 1760s by adding a separate condenser, doubling its efficiency.

twist.text: Watt's engines powered factories, trains, and ships. They replaced muscle power and changed the world.

climax.text: The study of how to improve steam engines gave birth to thermodynamics as a science.

punchline.text: Boil water, push a piston, change civilization. The steam engine started it all.

quiz.question: What improvement did James Watt make to the steam engine?
options: He added a separate condenser, greatly improving efficiency
He invented the first steam engine from scratch
